Of hebrew origin (g7343); Raab (i.e. Rachab), a Canaanitess

definition: From g4474 (ῥαπίζω); a slap

definition: From a primary (to sew; perhaps rather akin to the base of G4474 (ῥαπίζω) through the idea of puncturing); a needle

Of hebrew origin (h07354); Rachel, the wife of Jacob

Both prolonged forms of (which appears only in certain forms, and is itself probably a strengthened form of (see in g2608 (κατάγνυμι))) to "break," "wreck" or "crack", i.e. (especially) to sunder (by separation of the parts; G2608 (κατάγνυμι) being its intensive (with the preposition in composition), and G2352 (θραύω) a shattering to minute fragments; but not a reduction to the constituent particles, like G3089 (λύω)) or disrupt, lacerate; by implication, to convulse (with spasms); figuratively, to give vent to joyful emotions
From g4483 (ῥέω); an utterance (individually, collectively or specially),; by implication, a matter or topic (especially of narration, command or dispute); with a negative naught whatever
Probably of hebrew origin (apparently for h07509); Resa (i.e. Rephajah), an Israelite
From g4483 (ῥέω); a speaker, i.e. (by implication) a forensic advocate
Adverb from a derivative of g4483 (ῥέω); out-spokenly, i.e. distinctly
Apparently a primary word; a "root" (literally or figuratively)
From g4491 (ῥίζα); to root (figuratively, become stable)

From a derivative of g4496 (ῥίπτω) (meaning a fan or bellows); to breeze up, i.e. (by analogy) to agitate (into waves)
From a derivative of g4496 (ῥίπτω); to toss up

A primary verb (perhaps rather akin to the base of g4474 (ῥαπίζω), through the idea of sudden motion); to fling (properly, with a quick toss, thus differing from G906 (βάλλω), which denotes a deliberate hurl; and from (see in G1614 (ἐκτείνω)), which indicates an extended projection); by qualification, to deposit (as if a load); by extension, to disperse
Of hebrew origin (h07346); Roboam (i.e. Rechobam), an Israelite

Probably of foreign origin; a sabre, i.e. a long and broad cutlass (any weapon of the kind, literally or figuratively)
Of hebrew origin (h07205); Ruben (i.e. Reuben), an Israelite
Of hebrew origin (h07327); Ruth, a Moabitess

Prolongation from g4506 (ῥύομαι) in its original sense; an alley or avenue (as crowded)
Middle voice of an obsolete verb, akin to g4482 (ῥέω) (through the idea of a current; compare G4511 (ῥύσις)); to rush or draw (for oneself), i.e. rescue
From g4508 (ῥυπαρός); dirtiness (morally)
From g4509 (ῥύπος); dirty, i.e. (relatively) cheap or shabby; morally, wicked

From g4514 (ῥωμαῖος); Romaic, i.e. Latin
From g4516 (ῥώμη); Romæan, i.e. Roman (as noun)

From a derivative of g4521 (σάββατον); a "sabbatism", i.e. (figuratively) the repose of Christianity (as a type of heaven)

From a derivative of (to equip) meaning furniture, especially a pack-saddle (which in the east is merely a bag of netted rope); a "seine" for fishing

Of hebrew origin (h07597); Salathiel (i.e. Shealtiel), an Israelite

From g4561 (σάρξ); pertaining to flesh, i.e. (by extension) bodily, temporal, or (by implication) animal, unregenerate

Of hebrew origin (h08289); Saron (i.e. Sharon), a district of Palestine

From (seleucus, a syrian king); Seleuceia, a place in Syria
From (brilliancy; probably akin to the alternate of G138 (αἱρέομαι), through the idea of attractiveness); the moon

From (silence, i.e. a hush; properly, muteness, i.e. involuntary stillness, or inability to speak; and thus differing from G4602 (σιγή), which is rather a voluntary refusal or indisposition to speak, although the terms are often used synonymously); to be dumb (but not deaf also, like 2974 properly); figuratively, to be calm (as quiet water)
From g4625 (σκάνδαλον); to entrap, i.e. trip up (figuratively, stumble (transitively) or entice to sin, apostasy or displeasure)
("scandal"); probably from a derivative of g2578 (κάμπτω); a trap-stick (bent sapling), i.e. snare (figuratively, cause of displeasure or sin)

a "skiff" (as if dug out), or yawl (carried aboard a large vessel for landing)
Apparently from (to parch; through the idea of leanness); the leg (as lank)
From a derivative of (a covering; perhaps akin to the base of G4649 (σκοπός) through the idea of noticeableness); clothing

From g4632 (σκεῦος); furniture, i.e. spare tackle

Feminine of a compound of g4642 (σκληρός) and g2588 (καρδία); hard-heartedness, i.e. (specially), destitution of (spiritual) perception

Neuter of a presumed derivative of g1519 (εἰς) and g2965 (κύων) and g906 (βάλλω); what is thrown to the dogs, i.e. refuse (ordure)

From (sullen) and a derivative of g3700 (ὀπτάνομαι); angry-visaged, i.e. gloomy or affecting a mournful appearance
